doctrine-check.reason: The discipline is a direct consequence of already-ratified doctrine — mission §1.3 (function over category), the two-layer taxonomy, §0.5 (irreducibility), §3.2 (mapping-confidence), and held-tensions T2/T4/T9 — not a new doctrinal commitment; it names a working failure and forbids it. Owner-directed and ADOPTED, paired with the project-standards RULE per A12 CS8. No schema/DB/code change.

source-discipline.reason: No external literature claim is made. The factual content is (a) the three erasures, self-evident from this session's own transcript, and (b) the axis signatures they contradicted (POTS→STA+THR+BAL, CFS→STA, MCAS→CHM), which are rows in population_axis_map verified this session. The clinical distinctions cited (orthostatic vs exertional vs immune; dyspnea vs fatigue) are used to justify NOT-lumping and carry no threshold or spec claim requiring tier-anchoring.

adherence-logging-and-attestation.reason: PI rule #11 attestation for the new decision record; grounded at current doctrine 0f2f525.

bias_direction: The agent authored the errors, the critique of them, this corrective rule, and this attestation — and codifying its own failure as project doctrine could read as self-serving absolution (turning three mistakes into a virtuous 'guardrail' commit). It also expands the governance surface, which the anti-displacement discipline discourages. Bounded by: the owner explicitly directed the recording ('extremely important to your process') and the corrective framing ('work from axes'), so it is owner-mandated, not agent-initiated displacement; the rule constrains the agent's own future behaviour rather than granting it latitude; and it points to (does not resolve) the substantive reopenings (orthostatic/respiratory axes), leaving the real work undone and honestly flagged rather than claimed.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: A reviewer could argue this is process theatre: a rule that says 'don't lump' will not stop the next lump, and the same agent that lumped three times in two turns is unlikely to be saved by a RULE it wrote. Response: the guardrail is deliberately placed where it mechanically re-surfaces at the point of failure — CLAUDE.md §10 (read at session start) and the project-standards ledger (loaded often) and the taxonomy doctrine §3.3 (loaded exactly when population work happens) — and it converts a vague 'be careful' into a concrete stop-and-decompose trigger with named anti-examples; it is a necessary-not-sufficient control, and the real test is the reopened orthostatic/respiratory axis work being done axis-first, which this DR schedules rather than claims.
